raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need find files older than 2 days, delete and count. Use find with -mtime +2. Count with wc -l. But need delete and count. Could use find ... -mtime +2 -print -delete. But to count after deletion, we can capture list before deletion. Use find ... -mtime +2 -print | tee /dev/stderr | wc -l? Actually we want count of deleted files. We can use find ... -mtime +2 -print -delete | wc -l. But -delete will delete after printing. So pipeline: find ... -mtime +2 -print -delete | wc -l. That prints paths then deletes, then wc counts lines. So final command.
